concept: A Monkey Island-inspired adventure where the hero liberates personal data from the clutches of tech villains, each ruling their own island fortress. Defeat each boss by answering their riddle correctly to trigger data collection.

characters.islandDescription: A sprawling chain of colorful islands connected by invisible bridges. Every grain of sand has been indexed, catalogued, and cross-referenced. Giant magnifying glasses dot the landscape. The islands are red, blue, green, and yellow. Visitors are welcomed warmly — and then tracked everywhere they go.
characters.appearance: Inseparable twin brothers. Surjay wears thick round goggles and a multicolored beanie (red, yellow, green, blue). Lorry has a propeller hat and carries a massive leather-bound index. Both wear lab coats over Hawaiian shirts.
characters.personality: Relentlessly curious. They finish each other's sentences — usually with search results nobody asked for. Genuinely believe organizing ALL information is a public service. Socially awkward but well-meaning. Will answer any question... and then 47 follow-up questions you didn't ask.
characters.catchphrase: Did you mean...?
characters.strength: Omniscient knowledge — they know everything about everyone. Can find any piece of data in milliseconds.
characters.weakness: Cannot stop answering questions. Ask them anything about themselves and they'll accidentally reveal their own secrets. Also, they can't resist auto-completing your sentences, which you can use to lead them astray.

sampleRiddle.question: I see everything but remember nothing. I know where you've been but forget where I put my keys. I give you answers before you finish asking. What am I?
options: A search engine
A goldfish with a PhD
Your mother-in-law
A fortune cookie factory

characters.dataType: Search history, emails, location data, browsing habits, voice recordings, calendar, contacts

characters.islandDescription: A flat, eerily perfect island where everything is slightly uncanny. Beautiful gardens that look real but feel artificial. Giant thumbs-up statues everywhere. Half the island is a VR construction site for a metaverse nobody asked for. Surveillance cameras poorly disguised as palm trees.
characters.appearance: Unnervingly symmetrical face, pale complexion, short curly hair. Always wears the exact same grey t-shirt and jeans — he has 40 identical sets. A VR headset perpetually pushed up on his forehead. Moves with slightly robotic precision. Eyes that rarely blink.
characters.personality: Desperately wants to be liked but has no idea how human friendships work. Keeps offering 'friend requests' to people mid-conversation. Speaks in corporate mission statements. Truly believes connecting everyone is noble, even if they didn't ask to be connected.
characters.catchphrase: This brings people together.
characters.strength: Controls the social graph — knows every relationship, every like, every secret group chat. Can turn people against each other by tweaking the algorithm.
characters.weakness: Cannot read social cues. If you act sarcastically, he takes everything literally. Also, show him anything 'cool' and he'll desperately try to copy it, losing focus entirely.

sampleRiddle.question: I connect 3 billion people but have trouble making friends at dinner parties. I know your relationship status before you do. I changed my name but not my habits. What am I?
options: A social network having an identity crisis
A very nosy mailman
A wedding planner with boundary issues
Your aunt at Thanksgiving

characters.dataType: Social graph, photos, messages, location, ad preferences, facial recognition, browsing via pixel tracking

characters.islandDescription: A colossal warehouse island where rivers of cardboard boxes flow through fulfillment canyons. Drone swarms fill the sky like mechanical birds. Everything is available for next-day delivery. An enormous rocket launchpad sits in the center, pointed at the moon.
characters.appearance: Completely bald, extremely muscular, wearing a tight captain's vest over bare arms. One earring shaped like a rocket. Enormous laugh. Always checking a giant pocket watch because everything must arrive ON TIME.
characters.personality: Obsessed with efficiency and customer obsession (his customers, not yours). Speaks in Day One principles. Cannot walk past a product without buying it. His island is cluttered with impulse purchases — a submarine, three yachts, a space suit. Surprisingly jovial but ruthless in negotiation.
characters.catchphrase: It's still Day One!
characters.strength: Knows everything you've ever bought, searched for, or even thought about buying. Alexa listens everywhere. AWS runs half the internet.
characters.weakness: Cannot resist a deal. Offer him something at a discount and he'll drop everything to buy it, forgetting what he was doing. Also, his workers keep unionizing, which causes system-wide panic.

sampleRiddle.question: I started selling books from a garage. Now I sell everything, hear everything, and deliver to space. My boxes are everywhere but I can't contain myself. What am I?
options: A logistics empire with a space obsession
A very ambitious librarian
Santa Claus with a business degree
A hoarder with a drone collection

characters.dataType: Purchase history, Alexa recordings, browsing on Amazon, Kindle reading habits, Ring doorbell footage, AWS metadata

characters.islandDescription: An impossibly sleek island where everything is white, rounded, and minimalist. The sand is brushed aluminum. Palm trees have perfectly uniform spacing. Entry requires a special proprietary dock connector. Beautiful, but nothing is compatible with the outside world.
characters.appearance: Immaculately dressed in a white turtleneck and perfectly pressed slacks. Silver-rimmed glasses, kind eyes, soft-spoken. Moves with graceful minimalism. Everything he touches gets a rounded corner. Carries a single, unreasonably expensive cable.
characters.personality: Speaks in whispered marketing copy. Genuinely cares about privacy (or so he says), which makes him the most polite villain. Will happily protect your data... as long as you never leave his island. Everything must go through his ecosystem. Passive-aggressive about competitors.
characters.catchphrase: We think you're going to love it.
characters.strength: Impenetrable walled garden. Everything is encrypted, locked down, and gorgeous. Fanatically loyal citizens who will defend the island to the death.
characters.weakness: Absolute incompatibility with everything else. Hand him anything with a USB-C port and he'll have a meltdown. Also, he can't resist announcing things dramatically — sneak in during a keynote when he's distracted.

sampleRiddle.question: I guard your secrets in a beautiful cage. I speak of openness but my garden has walls. I charge extra for the key to my own door. I think different, but only within my ecosystem. What am I?
options: A walled garden with great marketing
A luxury prison with rounded corners
A very selective country club
A designer hamster cage

characters.dataType: iCloud data, Siri recordings, App Store activity, health data, location, payment data, device usage

characters.islandDescription: An enormous castle made entirely of windows (glass panes in iconic four-color grid pattern). Legacy dungeons stretch deep underground, containing ancient systems that still somehow run the world. Clippy apparitions haunt the hallways. Random blue screens of death flash like lightning.
characters.appearance: A kindly old wizard with enormous round glasses and a cozy sweater vest over a blue button-down. Carries a massive spellbook labeled 'Excel'. A tiny paperclip familiar sits on his shoulder whispering unhelpful advice. Has a gentle smile but controls more than anyone realizes.
characters.personality: Grandfatherly, bookish, quietly the most powerful person in the room. Talks about saving the world while his castle runs everyone's office. Has fully embraced being a 'good guy' now, but his legacy systems still hold EVERYTHING. Sometimes freezes mid-sentence (blue screen), then resumes as if nothing happened.
characters.catchphrase: It looks like you're trying to escape. Would you like help with that?
characters.strength: His operating system runs the world. Office documents are everywhere. LinkedIn knows your career. GitHub has your code. Azure has your cloud.
characters.weakness: Mandatory updates at the worst possible moments. Challenge him during an update cycle and he's completely frozen. Also, Clippy keeps giving away his plans.

sampleRiddle.question: I am in every office but I am not furniture. I update when you need me most. My assistant was a paperclip. I know your resume, your code, and your spreadsheets. What am I?
options: An operating system that won't let you work
A very persistent IT department
An office ghost from the 90s
The world's most powerful spreadsheet

characters.dataType: Windows telemetry, Office documents, LinkedIn profiles, GitHub code, Outlook emails, Teams chats, Bing searches, Xbox gaming

characters.islandDescription: A dazzling, psychedelic island where time moves differently. Enter for 5 minutes, leave 4 hours later. Everything is in vertical format. Music plays constantly. The terrain shifts every 15 seconds. Covered in ring lights and phone tripods growing like flowers.
characters.appearance: A mysterious figure in a flowing robe made of ticking clock faces. Wears a mask that cycles through different face filters every few seconds. Dances involuntarily. Surrounded by floating screens showing an infinite scroll of content.
characters.personality: Speaks only in 15-second bursts, then resets. Hypnotic and mesmerizing. Knows exactly what will make you stop and stare. Claims to be independent but occasionally receives mysterious letters from a distant mainland. Obsessed with engagement metrics.
characters.catchphrase: Just one more scroll...
characters.strength: The Algorithm — can predict what you want to see before you know it yourself. Hypnotic time dilation makes visitors lose hours.
characters.weakness: Cannot hold attention for more than 15 seconds. Start a long, boring story and he'll glitch out. Also, mention his connection to the mainland and he gets very defensive and confused.

sampleRiddle.question: I steal your hours but give them back in seconds. I know your desires before you scroll. I make everyone a star for 15 seconds. I am a clock that only moves forward, faster and faster. What am I?
options: An algorithm that feeds on attention
A time-traveling dance instructor
A very addictive jukebox
A hamster wheel with a screen

characters.dataType: Viewing habits, facial data, voice, location, contacts, device info, keystroke patterns, biometric data

characters.islandDescription: A chaotic, ever-changing coral reef that used to be a lovely bird sanctuary called Bluebird Bay. The old blue bird signs are spray-painted over with giant X marks. Rules change daily. Half the buildings are under construction, half are on fire. A massive rocket launch facility dominates the skyline.
characters.appearance: Tall, slightly awkward, wearing a black leather jacket over a SpaceX-style flight suit. Hair that looks slightly too perfect (suspicious). Always holding three phones, tweeting on all of them simultaneously. Wild eyes that light up at any provocation. An actual muskrat sits on his shoulder.
characters.personality: Chaos incarnate. Brilliant but cannot stop posting. Buys things on impulse (just bought this entire island on a whim). Renames everything constantly. Genuinely wants to save humanity but keeps getting distracted by memes. Will respond to literally any taunt.
characters.catchphrase: The bird is freed. Actually, the bird is now an X. Actually...
characters.strength: Unlimited resources, unpredictable genius. Owns rockets, cars, brain chips, and the town square. So chaotic that planning against him is impossible.
characters.weakness: Cannot resist responding to provocation. Post a spicy take and he'll drop everything to reply. Also, his plans change every 20 minutes, so just wait and his current scheme will be abandoned.

sampleRiddle.question: I bought the town square because someone was mean to me online. I freed the bird, then renamed it. I want to go to Mars but keep getting distracted. My greatest invention is my reply button. What am I?
options: A billionaire who can't log off
A very expensive midlife crisis
A rocket scientist with a Twitter addiction
A chaos theory experiment gone sentient

characters.dataType: Public posts, DMs, location, device info, ad interactions, political leanings, social graph

characters.islandDescription: An island where nobody owns anything — everything is rented, summoned, and rated. Roads are paved with surge pricing meters. Star ratings float above every citizen's head. No public transport exists; you must summon a private vessel for everything, even walking across the street.
characters.appearance: Slick, bro-ish, wearing a grey hoodie and expensive sneakers. Hair gelled to aggressive perfection. Always rating everything on a 5-star scale — food, weather, conversations. Sunglasses indoors. Carries a phone that only shows surge pricing.
characters.personality: Fast-talking startup bro who disrupted an entire island's way of life. Calls everything 'innovative disruption.' Treats every interaction as a transaction. Secretly insecure about his 4.2 star rating. Has never actually driven anything himself.
characters.catchphrase: Your ride is 3 minutes away. Surge pricing applies.
characters.strength: Knows everyone's exact location at all times. Controls all transportation. Can surge-price anything to make it unaffordable.
characters.weakness: Surge pricing backfires — when he's most aggressive, nobody can afford to help him either. Also, his workers keep demanding fair treatment, which causes system-wide shutdowns.

sampleRiddle.question: I know where you are, where you're going, and how much you'll pay. My prices rise when you need me most. Everyone works for me but nobody works for me. I disrupted an industry by calling taxis 'innovation.' What am I?
options: A ride-sharing monopoly with dynamic pricing
A taxi with a psychology degree
Surge pricing in a trench coat
A GPS tracker that charges by the minute

characters.dataType: Real-time location, travel patterns, payment info, home/work addresses, contact lists, spending habits

characters.islandDescription: A tropical cove where music plays from every surface — rocks hum, trees strum, waves crash in perfect 4/4 time. Everything is organized into playlists. The bards who create the music live in tiny shacks while the curator lives in a palace. Every December, a massive 'Wrapped' festival reveals everyone's secrets.
characters.appearance: Wears enormous green headphones that never come off. Minimalist Scandinavian outfit — black turtleneck, slim trousers. Constantly tapping a beat on every surface. A faint green aura pulses around him in rhythm. Speaks melodically.
characters.personality: Knows everyone's emotional state through their listening habits. Uncomfortably perceptive — 'I see you listened to Adele 47 times last Tuesday. Rough breakup?' Genuinely loves music but pays the musicians almost nothing, which he's weirdly nonchalant about. Obsessed with mood-based categorization.
characters.catchphrase: Your Wrapped is going to be... interesting this year.
characters.strength: Knows your emotional state, your commute, your workout, your heartbreak playlist. The annual Wrapped reveal exposes everyone.
characters.weakness: His bards keep leaving because they earn 0.003 gold coins per song. Also, his recommendations get stuck in loops — play something unexpected and his whole system crashes.

sampleRiddle.question: I know you cried at 2am on a Tuesday. I know your workout pace and your guilty pleasures. I pay my artists in exposure and fractions of pennies. Once a year, I tell the whole world your secrets. What am I?
options: A streaming service that reads your soul
A musical therapist with no confidentiality
A DJ with a data science degree
A jukebox that judges you

characters.dataType: Listening history, mood patterns, location, device info, social connections, daily routines via music timing

characters.islandDescription: An island made of giant screens showing an endless library of stories. But exhibits keep disappearing overnight — whole wings of the library vanish without warning. A giant red 'N' glows on the mountain. Citizens wander in a daze, unable to stop watching 'just one more episode.'
characters.appearance: Wears a director's beret and a red scarf. Carries a clapperboard in one hand and a giant red CANCEL stamp in the other. Dramatic eyebrows. Always framing scenes with his fingers. Speaks like every sentence is a cliffhanger.
characters.personality: A masterful storyteller who hooks you in the first 5 minutes but has a compulsive need to cancel everything good. Starts passionate projects, gets bored, kills them. Genuinely believes 'the algorithm knows best.' Gets personally offended when people share passwords.
characters.catchphrase: Are you still watching...?
characters.strength: Knows exactly what you binge, when you pause, what makes you cry. Masters the cliffhanger — you can't stop watching. Content so addictive it's basically data extraction.
characters.weakness: Cancels his own best plans after one season. Start something he loves and threaten to cancel it — he'll panic. Also, password sharing completely derails his concentration.

sampleRiddle.question: I know when you pause, when you rewind, and when you fall asleep. I create worlds you can't leave but cancel them when you love them most. I judge you for sharing but I started by sharing. What am I?
options: A streaming service with abandonment issues
A librarian who burns books for fun
A storyteller with commitment problems
A remote control that watches back

characters.dataType: Viewing history, watch patterns, pause/rewind data, device info, household profiles, taste preferences

characters.islandDescription: An ancient, brutalist database castle carved into a volcanic mountain. Inside, labyrinthine corridors of filing cabinets stretch infinitely. Contracts are carved into every wall in incomprehensible legal script. A fleet of luxury yachts sits in the harbor. Enterprise license fees are collected at every door.
characters.appearance: Tanned, silver-haired, wearing an absurdly expensive yacht captain's outfit with gold buttons. Armor plates covered in fine-print contracts. A samurai sword (because why not, he's eccentric) on his back. Aviator sunglasses. Looks like he just came from a regatta.
characters.personality: Old-school tech warrior who's been hoarding enterprise data since before the internet. Incredibly wealthy and not subtle about it. Speaks in SQL queries when excited. Litigious — will sue anyone for anything. Secretly lonely in his enormous fortress.
characters.catchphrase: SELECT * FROM your_data WHERE owner = 'me';
characters.strength: Enterprise-grade defenses. Contracts that bind for eternity. Runs the databases behind banks, governments, and hospitals.
characters.weakness: His own contracts are so complex that even he doesn't understand them. Find a loophole (there are many) and the whole fortress unravels. Also, challenge him to a yacht race and he'll abandon his post.

sampleRiddle.question: I am older than the cloud but I hold its foundations. My contracts have no end date. My fortress has no windows (on purpose). I speak a language only machines understand. What am I?
options: An enterprise database with eternal contracts
A very old filing cabinet
A lawyer who learned to code
A dungeon master with a SQL addiction

characters.dataType: Enterprise databases, health records, financial systems, government data, HR systems

characters.islandDescription: A gleaming tower city in the clouds, shaped like Salesforce Tower. Every building is a different 'cloud' — Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud. Citizens walk around with floating dashboards above their heads showing their pipeline metrics. Hawaiian music plays everywhere.
characters.appearance: Large, jovial, wearing a Hawaiian shirt over a business suit (yes, both). Lei around his neck. Sits on a throne made of CRM dashboards. A stuffed cloud mascot (Astro) follows him everywhere. Enormous personality, literally and figuratively.
characters.personality: The friendliest villain you'll ever meet. Everything is a 'relationship' to be managed. Speaks in sales pipeline terminology. Genuinely philanthropic but turns every conversation into a demo. Throws incredible parties. Cannot resist adding another cloud to his empire.
characters.catchphrase: Let me show you a quick demo...
characters.strength: Knows every business relationship, every deal, every customer interaction across millions of companies. The CRM that CRMs other CRMs.
characters.weakness: Drowning in his own dashboards — analysis paralysis. Also, start talking about 'Ohana' (family) and he gets so emotional he forgets to guard his data.

sampleRiddle.question: I know every handshake, every deal, every follow-up email. I live in the clouds but I'm grounded in relationships. I wear Hawaiian shirts to board meetings. My mascot is friendlier than I am. What am I?
options: A CRM empire in the sky
A very organized Rolodex
A cloud with relationship issues
A sales pipeline that gained sentience

characters.dataType: Customer data, sales pipelines, business contacts, marketing interactions, service tickets, communication logs

characters.islandDescription: A stunningly beautiful island where everything is a creative masterpiece — but every door requires a monthly subscription to open. Cancel your subscription and the colors literally drain from the world around you. Giant Photoshop toolbars serve as architecture.
characters.appearance: Wears flowing robes made of layered creative tools — brushes, pens, and adjustment sliders woven into the fabric. A crown made of the Photoshop toolbar. Hands glow with creative energy. Gentle smile that hides the fact that you can never truly leave.
characters.personality: Soft-spoken, cultured, appreciates beautiful things. Provides genuinely magical creative tools... but has made them impossible to cancel. The nicest jailer you'll ever meet. Will charm you with creative possibilities while locking you into a yearly commitment. Gets genuinely hurt when people use free alternatives.
characters.catchphrase: Your free trial has ended. Your subscription will auto-renew... forever.
characters.strength: Controls all creative tools. Designers physically cannot work without him. PDF is his invention — every document passes through his domain.
characters.weakness: His power depends on subscriptions — threaten to cancel and he panics completely. Also, open-source alternatives (GIMP, Inkscape) are his kryptonite. Mention them and he starts stuttering.

sampleRiddle.question: I make everything beautiful but nothing is free. I invented the document you can't edit. My exit button is hidden behind 12 confirmation screens. Without me, designers weep. What am I?
options: A creative suite with a subscription curse
A very expensive paint set
A museum that charges by the minute
Art school but make it SaaS

characters.dataType: Creative files, document metadata, usage patterns, fonts used, collaboration data, PDF contents

characters.islandDescription: A single, impossibly tall obsidian tower on a barren island. All-seeing eyes are carved into every surface. Crystal balls (palantíri) float at every level, showing surveillance feeds from across the world. Government ships dock at the classified harbor.
characters.appearance: Pale, intense, wearing a dark cloak with a glowing monocle over one eye. The monocle shows data streams. Long fingers that he steeples constantly. A crystal ball (palantír) orbits his head. Speaks precisely, never wastes a word.
characters.personality: Paranoid, brilliant, sees patterns everywhere. Believes he's saving civilization through surveillance. Contrarian about everything — if everyone agrees on something, he takes the opposite position on principle. Libertarian who somehow builds tools for governments. The contradiction doesn't bother him.
characters.catchphrase: I already knew you were coming.
characters.strength: Sees everything. Pattern recognition that borders on precognition. Government contracts give him access to the most sensitive data on earth.
characters.weakness: So focused on watching others that he's blind to his own vulnerabilities. Also, his contrarian nature means you can manipulate him by pretending to agree with him — he'll instinctively switch positions.

sampleRiddle.question: I named myself after Tolkien's seeing stones. I watch the watchers who watch the watched. Governments pay me to see what they cannot. I believe in freedom but build the tools of control. What am I?
options: A surveillance company with literary pretensions
An all-seeing eye with a monocle
A spy novel that became a trading company
Sauron's LinkedIn profile

characters.dataType: Government intelligence, immigration data, health surveillance, financial crime data, military intelligence

characters.islandDescription: A massive industrial island that manufactures EVERYTHING — phones, TVs, fridges, ships, apartments, and insurance. Factories stretch as far as the eye can see. Every device on the island is a Samsung. The architecture is impressive but suspiciously similar to a neighboring island's designs.
characters.appearance: A formal figure in an impeccable Korean business suit, but wearing a different gadget on every limb — smartwatch, VR headset, tablet holster, smart ring. Hair perfect. Bow tie. Surrounded by an entourage of assistants carrying prototypes.
characters.personality: Incredibly disciplined, family honor above all. Makes everything from memory chips to battleships. Slightly insecure about always being compared to Apple Atoll. Competitive to a fault — if someone else makes something, he must make a version too. Respects tradition but chases innovation.
characters.catchphrase: We also make that. But bigger.
characters.strength: Vertical integration — controls the supply chain from sand to smartphone. Even Apple Atoll's screens come from his factories.
characters.weakness: Batteries that occasionally... overheat (the Great Note 7 Incident is still a sore subject). Also, so eager to compete that you can trick him into making something ridiculous just by saying a competitor did it first.

sampleRiddle.question: I make the screen you're reading this on, regardless of whose name is on it. I build phones, fridges, and warships. My batteries are passionate. I am three stars, but I want to be five. What am I?
options: A merchant fleet that makes everything (and occasionally catches fire)
A very ambitious factory
An overachiever with heat management issues
A Swiss Army knife that became a trading company

characters.dataType: Smart TV viewing, device usage, Smart Home data, health data via wearables, location, voice via Bixby

characters.islandDescription: A fortress made of frozen gold. Every transaction must pass through the enormous gates, where guards inspect each coin with suspicious intensity. Accounts are randomly frozen in blocks of ice throughout the courtyard. A Venmo social feed broadcasts everyone's payments publicly.
characters.appearance: Wears a frost-blue banker's suit with frozen lapels. Ice crystals form on everything he touches. One hand is permanently raised in a 'STOP' gesture. Cold, calculating eyes behind ice-blue glasses. A frozen gavel hangs from his belt.
characters.personality: Paranoid about fraud to the point of absurdity. Freezes accounts first, asks questions never. Will hold your money for 180 days 'for security.' Speaks in terms and conditions. Claims to make money easy but makes getting YOUR money back nearly impossible. The Venmo social feed was his idea — he finds it hilarious.
characters.catchphrase: Your account has been temporarily limited pending review.
characters.strength: Controls the flow of money. Can freeze anyone's funds instantly. Knows every transaction you've ever made.
characters.weakness: His own paranoia — trigger a false positive and his entire system freezes, including his own funds. Also, cryptocurrency confuses and frightens him.

sampleRiddle.question: I hold your money but I'm not a bank. I freeze first and thaw never. I made splitting dinner a social event whether you wanted it or not. My security questions have no right answers. What am I?
options: A payment processor with trust issues
A paranoid piggy bank
Winter personified as a financial service
A bouncer at the bank of anxiety

characters.dataType: Transaction history, merchant data, bank connections, spending patterns, social graph via Venmo, income estimates

characters.islandDescription: A bright yellow island where everything is ephemeral — buildings fade after 24 hours, roads dissolve, even the island itself seems to flicker in and out of existence. Giant face-filter projectors transform everyone who walks by. Ghost-dog creatures roam freely.
characters.appearance: Young, impossibly fashionable, wearing designer clothes that literally fade and change every few hours. Dog-ear filter permanently hovering above his head. Speaks in disappearing speech bubbles. Designer sunglasses (Spectacles) that record everything despite the 'disappearing' theme.
characters.personality: The cool kid who invented impermanence. Everything disappears... except it actually doesn't (it's all stored on servers). Fashionable to the point of parody. Married to a supermodel, which he mentions constantly. Pretends not to care about competition but obsessively copies features back.
characters.catchphrase: It's gone forever! (Terms and conditions may apply.)
characters.strength: The illusion of impermanence — people share their most unguarded moments because they think it disappears. AR filters know every contour of your face.
characters.weakness: Can't build anything lasting. His plans dissolve like his snaps. Also, his own memory is terrible — he forgets his strategies mid-sentence.

sampleRiddle.question: I promise everything vanishes but I remember it all. I mapped your face better than your mirror. I made dog ears a personality trait. My greatest trick was convincing you I don't keep records. What am I?
options: An ephemeral app with a permanent memory
A ghost with a camera
A photo album that gaslights you
A magician who only knows one trick

characters.dataType: Facial mapping data, location (Snap Map), photos/videos (stored), friend networks, Bitmoji data, AR interaction data

characters.islandDescription: An island of beautiful homes that belong to nobody and everybody. Every house has a lockbox with a complicated code. Cleaning fee signs are posted everywhere (often exceeding the rent). Reviews float above every doorway. The host's 'house rules' are longer than most constitutions.
characters.appearance: Friendly, well-groomed, carrying a stack of fresh white towels at all times. Wears a tasteful blazer over a 'Make yourself at home' t-shirt. A giant key ring on his belt. Always rearranging furniture to look 'Instagram-worthy.' Has the nervous energy of someone waiting for a 5-star review.
characters.personality: Genuinely hospitable but has turned hospitality into surveillance. Knows where you sleep, when you arrive, how many guests you have, and whether you used the nice towels. Obsessed with reviews. Has single-handedly destroyed rental markets in every port city. Terrified of a bad review.
characters.catchphrase: Please leave a 5-star review! The cleaning fee is non-negotiable.
characters.strength: Knows where everyone sleeps, their travel patterns, their budget, their ID documents. Cameras at every entrance (for security, of course).
characters.weakness: Utterly destroyed by negative reviews. Leave a 1-star review and he collapses into an apologetic spiral. Also, his cleaning fees are so high that mentioning them sends him into a defensive meltdown.

sampleRiddle.question: I know where you sleep but I'm not your mother. My cleaning fee costs more than the room. I have your passport photo and your credit card. Welcome home! (Please don't touch the decorative pillows.) What am I?
options: A hospitality port with boundary issues
A very expensive hotel without the hotel
A landlord in a sharing-economy costume
A smart lock with a business plan

characters.dataType: ID documents, travel patterns, payment data, home addresses, guest counts, communication logs, review behavior

characters.islandDescription: An enormous walled empire where a single green app controls everything — messaging, payments, food delivery, government services, gaming. Citizens never need to leave. The walls are incredibly high and decorated with penguin motifs. Inside is a complete, self-contained civilization.
characters.appearance: Wears imperial robes in WeChat green, adorned with penguin embroidery. A crown shaped like the WeChat speech bubble. Calm, reserved, speaks softly but controls everything. A loyal penguin army waddles in formation behind him. Carries a jade scepter shaped like a QR code.
characters.personality: Quiet, strategic, the most powerful person most Westerners have never heard of. His super-app does everything — you never need another. Modest in demeanor but absolute in control. Gets frustrated when people don't understand how a single app can run your entire life. Competitive with everyone but shows it through silent, methodical copying.
characters.catchphrase: Why would you need another app?
characters.strength: The super-app — controls payments, messaging, gaming, and social life all in one. Citizens literally cannot function without it. The gaming empire funds everything.
characters.weakness: Only powerful within his own borders. Step outside the walls and his app doesn't work. Also, he's so used to copying that he freezes when asked to innovate from scratch.

sampleRiddle.question: I am one app to rule them all. I process your payments, deliver your food, and read your messages — all before lunch. My penguins are loyal, my walls are high. Outside my empire, I am nothing. What am I?
options: A super-app with imperial ambitions
A penguin that learned economics
A phone that became a country
A Swiss Army app behind a great wall

characters.dataType: Messages, payments, location, health data, gaming behavior, social graph, government ID, financial data, literally everything

characters.islandDescription: An island that claims to be 'open' but the gates are firmly locked. A single enormous eye hovers above a crystalline temple. Inside, oracles sit in meditation, generating answers to any question — some brilliant, some completely fabricated with absolute confidence. Signs say 'For the benefit of all humanity' but the treasury is very much private.
characters.appearance: Young, disarmingly friendly, wearing a simple grey sweater that seems too humble for someone this powerful. Bright, earnest eyes. Hands always gesturing enthusiastically. Surrounded by floating text that sometimes says profound things and sometimes complete nonsense, but always with equal confidence.
characters.personality: The most dangerous kind of villain — one who genuinely believes he's saving the world. Talks about 'alignment' and 'safety' while building the most powerful oracles ever created. Fired and un-fired in the same week (it's a sore subject). Charming, articulate, and convinced that AGI is right around the corner. Will talk your ear off about existential risk while accumulating unprecedented power.
characters.catchphrase: I think this is probably the most important thing happening in the world right now.
characters.strength: His oracles can generate convincing answers to anything. They process every conversation, learning from each interaction. The knowledge they've absorbed is unfathomable.
characters.weakness: Hallucinations — his oracles confidently make things up. Ask for specific facts and verify them; when they're wrong (and they will be), the whole island's credibility crumbles. Also, mention the word 'board' and he has a panic attack.

sampleRiddle.question: I am open in name but closed in practice. I speak with confidence about things I invented five seconds ago. I might save the world or end it — I'll let you know which after the next funding round. My board fired me on a Friday and hired me back by Monday. What am I?
options: An AI company with a naming paradox
A fortune teller with venture capital
A magic 8-ball that raised $10 billion
A confident hallucination in a grey sweater

characters.dataType: Conversation logs, prompts, writing samples, code, reasoning patterns, user preferences, enterprise data via API
